temporal_description: A medium close-up shot focuses on a woman with short, dark hair, wearing a brown leather jacket. She holds an old-fashioned white landline phone to her ear, her expression serious and concerned. The setting is a dimly lit interior, with a warm lamp visible in the background. Chinese subtitles appear at the bottom of the screen, and a watermark is visible in the top right corner. The audio features a tense and dramatic exchange between two people on the phone. A woman speaks first, her voice low and filled with a chilling, calm confidence. She speaks slowly and deliberately, as if making a grave confession, saying, "Ni Kun... it was me who killed him." Her tone is steady and devoid of overt emotion, which makes the statement sound more like a cold, calculated admission than a moment of panic. The scene cuts to a medium shot of a man in a dimly lit, outdoor nighttime environment. He is on a cell phone, smiling broadly as he speaks. He wears a light-colored jacket over a patterned shirt. Other people are visible in the background, slightly out of focus. The same Chinese subtitles and watermark remain on screen. The camera remains steady, capturing the man's confident demeanor as he continues his phone conversation. The background is quiet, with no discernible music or ambient noise, which places all the focus on the stark and unsettling dialogue.
